name: Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social
header_text: Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social
section_which_is_best_for_you:
This article offers a detailed breakdown of Buffer and Loomly, both social media management platforms. We delve into their features, benefits, pros, and cons to help you choose the right platform for your agency. Since we’re focusing on agencies, we’ll also explore the features of Synup Social, an agency-first platform.

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: At a glance!

Buffer is a social media management tool that helps businesses and individuals schedule posts, analyze performance, and manage multiple social media accounts from one dashboard. It offers publishing, engagement, analytics, and team collaboration features to streamline social media efforts.

Loomly is a social media management tool that helps teams and individuals plan, create, and schedule posts across multiple channels. It offers content creation tools, approval workflows, scheduling, and analytics to streamline social media efforts and track performance. Caters to agencies as well.

Synup Social is an agency-first platform where you can organize, publish, oversee, and create your content across major social media platforms such as Twitter, Instagram, Facebook, Pinterest, Linkedin, YouTube, etc. It is also an end-to-end AI and API-enabled platform that helps manage your social media content effortlessly.
section_which_should_you_choose:
Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: Social Media Management 

Regarding Social Media Management, Synup is the most affordable option for agencies compared to the other two with its enhanced AI capabilities and a full-fledged agency-enabled platform. It helps with creating, publishing, scheduling, and monitoring social media activity across platforms and comes with in-built social listening tools.

In comparison, Buffer offers similar services, such as creating, posting, scheduling, and a plethora of other resources to help agencies manage their social media accounts. It also comes with an additional AI writing feature that will help you create posts on a whim, however, lacks certain features such as AI-assisted posting and creating top and trending posts with just a single click. 

Source: Buffer

Loomly does not offer any AI capabilities when it comes to Social Media Management, therefore, offers basic features such as posting, creating, and monitoring content across various platforms. However, it does offer hashtag suggestions and various other integrations to help come up with content ideas. 

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: Social Analytics 

Buffer offers a good set of analytical tools for its social media analytics, that helps track performance and create reports for Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, and Linkedin all from a single dashboard. However, customers say that the platform does not offer social listening and monitoring, that feels lacking. 

Loomly offers basic analytical features, say a lot of users making use of Loomly. It also offers premium analytical features that are paid and cost extra. Also, customers feel that the data is not very granular, especially when it comes to tools such as Snapchat and TikTok, where it needs to improve. 

Synup offers comprehensive analytical features, suggesting the best times to post, and a detailed summary of your social media accounts and shows comparison to previous periods with flexible date range options. It also comes with an interactive graph that tracks social trends and can view posts that affect engagement and do more. Synup’s intuitive follower growth graph helps you track your follower growth across various platforms within a specific period. All these analytics can also be downloaded as reports easily via the Synup dashboard.

Source: Synup

‍

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: AI Capabilities 

Synup is a completely AI-enabled platform. It offers various AI capabilities for content generation, hashtag generation, and AI-assisted posting that help agencies stay on track with their social media calendars and create more content seamlessly. It also helps by generating instant Instagram posts and staying on track with Twitter in just a single click, along with AI-assisted brand-specific post creation that helps you generate content for international and national holidays. These features make it easy for agencies to stay on track with their content goals. 

Source: Review sourced and collected by G2

‍

Buffer comes with its own set of AI capabilities that it has to offer such as brainstorming ideas, repurposing content, creating and generating content at the click of a button, and getting more social media post suggestions from long-form content, which makes it very easy for creators, agencies and freelancers to post and keep up with their content calendars. 

Loomly does not offer many AI capabilities that help with content creation, however, for inspiration, users tend to use various other third-party tools that have been offered by the platform. Though, because of this, users tend to feel that they are missing out on AI capabilities when working with Loomly for their social media requirements.

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: Integrations

Synup offers a wide array of integrations and is a clear winner when it comes to Integrations! It offers coverage across major social media platforms such as Linkedin, Pinterest, Instagram, Twitter, and Facebook and along with that, it comes with various other integrations such as MailChimp, Airtable, Microsoft Teams, Clover, Square, Asana, and a lot of others to help make it easier for agencies to create and schedule content. While at the same time, making use of these tools as third-party integrations that can take their team communication and collaboration to the next level. 

Buffer offers various integrations such as Canva, Giphy, DropBox, Google Photos, Instagram Stories Creator, and more to help with content creation. However, as compared to Synup, these integrations are very limited. Users also say that these integrations can help with content creation but offer limited features. Another drawback is that Buffer does not offer Instagram as a publisher network in its basic plan.

Loomly has basic integrations with Canva, Giphy, Google Drive, and Unsplash for content creation along with Zapier, Slack, and Microsoft Teams to enable collaboration amongst teams and make it easier for them to communicate better. 

Source: Loomly

‍

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: Reseller Support

Synup stands out when it comes to Reseller Support as opposed to Buffer and Loomly. Both Loomly and Buffer offer basic features such as white labeling dashboards and reports, however, Synup on the other hand takes it up a notch by offering managed services for agencies and offering full-fledged white labeling capabilities. 

Additionally, agencies also get to enjoy various other features that Synup offers, such as a scan tool that helps you show your customers that you’re the right choice. Synup also helps gain valuable insights and a tailored strategy for all the prospects with a single click. 

With that, agencies also get dedicated training, support, and early access to products that Synup launches. This is exactly what makes Synup the number one tool for agencies and reseller support. 

Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: Which one should you choose? 

Buffer is great for SMBs or Freelancers looking to add an extra layer of creativity with a few additional features and don’t have a lot of budget to spare on Social Media. However, there’s nothing that Synup can’t handle!

Loomly is great for Medium-sized and Large Companies looking to manage their end-to-end social media accounts and keep on track with their monthly content goals. But, if you’re someone who is looking for full-fledged Local SEO and Social Media Management options, there’s nothing like Synup. 

Synup offers a full-fledged solution for agencies with everything right from Social Media to Local SEO, at their fingertips and It is highly recommended for agencies, VSMBs, and Mid-sized and Large-sized businesses. 

‍

Summing up

While zeroing down on a Social Media Management Tool, start by evaluating your business needs and social media goals. List essential features like scheduling and content planning, then compare tools based on their price-to-value ratio. Think about both your current and future social platforms. For multi-location businesses, prioritize tools that manage Google Business Profile updates and other listings. This way, you’ll choose a tool that aligns with your strategy and offers room for growth.

‍
faq_rich_text:
Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup Social: FAQs

  1. Is Loomly better than Buffer?

When comparing Loomly and Buffer, Buffer excels in post-scheduling and analytics, while Loomly offers advanced collaboration and content ideas. However, Synup stands out as the all-in-one solution, covering social media management, local listings, reviews, and comprehensive analytics, making it ideal for businesses looking to streamline their online presence.

  1. Is Loomly as good as Hootsuite?

Loomly and Hootsuite each have their strengths: Hootsuite offers comprehensive tools and analytics, while Loomly excels in user-friendly content planning and collaboration. However, Synup distinguishes itself by not only handling social media management but also managing local listings, reviews, and detailed analytics, making it a top choice for businesses needing a broad online presence.

  1. Does Loomly have a social inbox feature? 

Loomly does have a social inbox feature. It allows users to manage and respond to messages, comments, and mentions from multiple social media platforms in one place. This feature helps streamline communication and engagement with your audience, making it easier to track and address interactions effectively.

‍

seo.title: Buffer v/s Loomly v/s Synup, which one should you choose in 2024?
seo.description: Looking for a Social Media Management platform and confused Buffer, Loomly and Synup? Here’s a detailed breakdown for you.
